LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT JEFFREY PERRY'S MOTION TO LIFT DISCOVERY STAY AND CONDUCT SPECIFIED DISCOVERY. DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT PERRY'S MOTION TO LIFT DISCOVERY STAY IS DENIED. (SEE TUTOR-SALIBA CORP. V. HERRERA (2006) 136 CAL.APP.4TH 604, 617 [DENYING DISCOVERY REQUEST].)DEFENDANT FAILS TO ESTABLISH GOOD CAUSE FOR THE DISCOVERY REQUEST. PERRY DENIES THE SEXUAL ASSAULT CHARGES AND THEREFORE, CAN ESTABLISH FALSITY TO SUPPORT THE DEFAMATION CLAIMS WITHOUT INFORMATION FROM ROGERS. PERRY IS ALSO PRIVY TO A TEXT WHERE ROGERS ALLEGEDLY STATED THAT SHE "HATES" PERRY. (MOVING POINTS AND AUTHORITIES ("MP&A"), 2:11-12; ROGERS DECL., EX. A (10/26/23).) CONDUCTING DISCOVERY REGARDING ROGERS' INVESTIGATION INTO THE CLAIMED SEXUAL ASSAULTS WOULD BE CUMULATIVE EVIDENCE OF MALICE. IN ADDITION, THE REQUESTED DISCOVERY IS OVERBROAD TO OPPOSE THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION. (MP&A AT 4:10-21.) PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR. , CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

Off Calendar Sep-24-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT ALEXANDRA ROGERS' DEMURRER to 1ST Amended CROSS COMPLAINT.

Alexandra Rogers' demurrer to Jeffrey Perry's first amended cross-complaint is overruled without prejudice. CCP 430.60 provides: "A demurrer shall distinctly specify the grounds upon which any of the objections to the cross-complaint are taken. Unless it does so, it may be disregarded." Here, Rogers merely "generally demurrers [sic] to all cause of actions [sic]." Also: "Each ground of demurrer must be in a separate paragraph and must state whether it applies to the entire?cross-complaint...or to specified causes of action" (CRC 3.1320(a).)

Order is signed.

MOTION TO LIFT DISCOVERY STAY AND CONDUCT SPECIFIED DISCOVERY
Off Calendar Dec-06-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT JEFFREY PERRY'S MOTION TO LIFT DISCOVERY STAY AND CONDUCT SPECIFIED DISCOVERY.

Defendant/cross-complainant Perry's motion to lift discovery stay is denied. (See Tutor-Saliba Corp. v. Herrera (2006) 136 Cal.App.4th 604, 617 [denying discovery request].)

Defendant fails to establish good cause for the discovery request. Perry denies the sexual assault charges and therefore, can establish falsity to support the defamation claims without information from Rogers. Perry is also privy to a text where Rogers allegedly stated that she "hates" Perry. (Moving points and authorities ("MP&A"), 2:11-12; Rogers Decl., Ex. A (10/26/23).) Conducting discovery regarding Rogers' investigation into the claimed sexual assaults would be cumulative evidence of malice. In addition, the requested discovery is overbroad to oppose the anti-SLAPP motion. (MP&A at 4:10-21.)

Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. , Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/RBU)
